title: Flexbox layout
summary: Flexbox distributes space along one axis (row or column). Ideal for navigation bars, toolbars, and centering. Effectively the default 1D layout system since full browser support.
content:
## Main concepts

- **flex-direction** — row vs column.
- **justify-content** — alignment along the main axis.
- **align-items** — alignment on the cross axis.
- **flex-grow/shrink/basis** — how items grow or shrink.

## Gotchas

Min-width defaults can prevent shrinking—sometimes `min-width: 0` on flex children fixes overflow.

## Era

Post-2020 projects typically choose **Flex + Grid** instead of float/clearfix layouts.

related_questions.preview: Flexbox vs Grid — when to pick each
Gap vs margins in flex layouts

title: CSS Grid
summary: Grid lays out items in rows and columns simultaneously—great for dashboards, galleries, and page shells. Widely adopted in the ~2020 “modern layout” era alongside Flexbox.
content:
## Tracks

Define columns and rows with `grid-template-columns` / `rows`. `fr` distributes **available** space.

## Placement

Use named areas (`grid-template-areas`) for readability, or line-based placement for precision.

## Classic pattern

`repeat(3, 1fr)` builds equal columns—ideal for card grids and app shells.

title: The if() function (conditional values)
summary: Emerging CSS lets you pick computed values from a condition—reducing duplicate rules for themes and preferences. Syntax and support are still stabilizing; treat as progressive enhancement.
content:
## Idea

Instead of repeating `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` blocks for every property, **conditional value functions** can inline the branch where the spec allows.

## Mindset

CSS already had “logic” via cascade, media queries, and `:has()`—`if()` makes some branches **explicit at value level**. Check **current** browser docs before shipping.

## Further reading

Overview of fast-moving CSS features (popular write-up): [CSS in 2025–2026 on DEV](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8).

code_examples.title: Conceptual (verify syntax in MDN / spec)

title: Native masonry layout (Grid)
summary: Browser-native masonry-style layouts reduce reliance on JS libraries for uneven-height columns. Availability differs by engine—feature-detect or provide a dense Grid fallback.
content:
## Why it matters

Classic Pinterest-like columns often needed **Masonry.js** or fragile scripts. Native masonry aims for **paint-stable**, scrollable columns tied to Grid.

## Practical note

Property names and availability evolve—test **Chrome Canary / Firefox Nightly** flags vs stable. Fallback: multi-column layout or JS until baseline widens.

## Reference

Discussed among **2025–2026 CSS** roundups such as [this DEV article](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8).

code_examples.title: Illustrative Grid masonry (verify current keywords)

title: Anchor positioning
summary: Anchor positioning ties floating UI (tooltips, menus) to anchor elements with less manual JS measuring—pairs well with Popover and semantic buttons.
content:
## Problem

Absolutely positioning tooltips relative to triggers usually needs **resize/scroll listeners** or libraries.

## Direction

Declare **anchors** and **positioned** elements that reference them so the browser keeps alignment when layout shifts.

## Support

Cutting-edge—verify Baseline and ship fallbacks. Same topic area as popular **2025–2026 CSS** summaries ([DEV overview](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8)).

code_examples.title: Conceptual pattern (names vary by spec level)

title: text-box-trim & box metrics
summary: Fine-grained control over extra vertical space around glyph boxes—helps align mixed fonts and icons without optical clipping hacks.
content:
## Why

Line boxes include **half-leading** and font metrics that make “perfect” vertical alignment with icons frustrating.

## Direction

Properties like **`text-box-trim`** (alongside related metrics work) aim to trim or align to **cap height / alphabetic baseline** more predictably.

## Support

Emerging—pair with progressive enhancement. Mentioned in forward-looking CSS roundups ([example](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8)).

title: Scroll-driven animations
summary: Drive animations from scroll position or element visibility without imperative scroll listeners—lighter bundles when baseline matches your targets.
content:
## Building blocks

**Scroll timelines** and **view timelines** connect `@keyframes` to scroll progress—fade/slide as sections enter. Older discussions referenced **`@scroll-timeline`**; modern APIs center on **`animation-timeline`** + named scroll/view timelines.

## A11y

Respect **`prefers-reduced-motion`**—disable or shorten motion.

## Era

Often grouped with **2024+** “smarter CSS” features—animate from scroll without JS when baseline allows.

## Further reading

Featured in “CSS getting too powerful” style articles ([DEV](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8)).

title: Native CSS mixins & functions (roadmap)
summary: The ecosystem is exploring native reuse patterns beyond custom properties—reducing preprocessor dependency for simple repeated declarations. Shipping shape TBD; verify specs.
content:
## Context

Preprocessors (Sass `@mixin`) historically filled reuse gaps. **Standard CSS** continues to add **functions**, `@apply`-style discussions, and mixin-like proposals—exact syntax lands in CSS WG drafts.

## Advice

Use **design tokens** (`--token`) + `@layer` today; migrate to native mixins when Baseline fits your audience.

## Casual overview

[CSS in 2025–2026 discussion](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8) highlights Sass-level ergonomics coming closer to the platform.

code_examples.title: Preprocessor analogy (not native syntax)

title: margin-trim
summary: Trim sibling margins at container edges so first/last blocks don’t double up with parent padding—cleaner vertical rhythm without spacer divs.
content:
## Problem

**Margin collapse** surprises spacing at section boundaries.

## Direction

`margin-trim` (values like **block / inline / all**) tells the container to absorb or trim margins at specified sides—fewer “mystery gaps.”

## Support

Check Baseline before relying site-wide. Mentioned alongside other layout QoL features in [this 2025–2026 CSS article](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8).

title: contrast-color()
summary: Compute accessible foreground colors against a background automatically—fewer manual contrast checks for themed surfaces.
content:
## Goal

WCAG contrast math belongs in the engine so **tokens + surfaces** stay readable when users pick accent colors.

## Usage mindset

Pair with **`color-mix()`**, **`light-dark()`**, and system colors for resilient themes.

## Support

Emerging—verify Safari/Chrome/Firefox tables. Discussed in accessibility-forward CSS summaries ([DEV piece](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8)).

title: progress() & scroll-linked values
summary: Bridge scroll ranges into interpolations—think opacity tied to scroll segment without reading `scrollY` in JS when supported.
content:
## Idea

Expose **progress along an axis** (scroll, timelines) to functions that output lengths or numbers for butter-smooth transitions.

## Practical pairing

Works conceptually with **scroll-driven animations** and reduced-motion policies.

## Reference

Highlighted among futuristic CSS APIs in [CSS 2025–2026 commentary](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8)—always verify syntax in spec snapshots.

code_examples.title: Illustrative (verify function signature)

title: View Transitions API
summary: Animate between DOM states or navigations with browser-managed snapshots—shared-element morphing without framework-specific routers.
content:
## Flow

`document.startViewTransition(() => updateDOM())` captures old/new views; CSS **`::view-transition-*`** pseudo-elements style the transition.

## Scope

Works for **MPA** navigations in supporting browsers plus SPA state changes—still progressive enhancement.

## Further reading

Popularized as “Pixar-smooth” page changes in articles like [this DEV overview](https://dev.to/pixelperfect_pro/css-in-2025-2026-its-getting-too-powerful-and-im-scared-2ej8).

title: CSS variables (custom properties)
summary: Custom properties (`--name`) cascade and inherit—foundation for design tokens, themes, and runtime theming without preprocessor builds.
content:
## Basics

Declare on `:root` or any selector; read with **`var(--name, fallback)`**. Values resolve at computed-value time.

## Why teams adopted them (~2020+)

One stylesheet can swap entire palettes via a class on `<html>` or `[data-theme]`.

## Pairs with

`color-mix()`, **`light-dark()`**, container queries, and `@layer` for maintainable systems.

title: clamp() — fluid values
summary: `clamp(min, preferred, max)` keeps typography and spacing between bounds—fewer breakpoints for fluid UIs.
content:
## Pattern

Preferred often mixes **vw + rem** so text scales smoothly.

## Use cases

Font sizes, gaps, icon boxes, anything that should breathe between mobile and desktop.

## Timeline

Landing page CSS talks (~2020 onward) popularized clamp-heavy **fluid design**.

title: Container queries
summary: Style by **container size**, not viewport—components adapt inside sidebars, modals, and dashboards.
content:
## Setup

Mark a wrapper with **`container-type`** (often `inline-size`). Query with **`@container (min-width: …)`**.

## Shift

**Component-responsive** design vs media-query-only layouts—a major ~2022–2023 workflow change.

## Fallback

Mobile-first `@media` still helps when containers aren’t supported everywhere you ship.

title: :has() parent selector
summary: Match an element when it contains another—style the parent from child state without extra JS classes.
content:
## Power

Previously awkward patterns (empty states, focus-within menus) become declarative.

## Performance

Avoid overly broad `:has()` on huge trees; test interaction with **forgiving selector lists**.

## Era

Often cited as a **game changer** alongside container queries in the component-CSS wave.

title: @layer — cascade layers
summary: Explicit layer order (`base`, `components`, `utilities`) replaces fighting specificity wars—better CSS architecture without endless `!important`.
content:
## Usage

Declare order once: `@layer base, components, utilities;` then wrap rules in `@layer utilities { … }`.

## Wins

Predictable overrides for design systems and CSS reset vs component styles.

## Pairing

Works with **custom properties**, `@scope`, and preprocessors less often needed for ordering hacks.

title: Dynamic viewport units (dvh, svh, lvh)
summary: **svh / lvh / dvh** describe small/large/dynamic viewport height as mobile UI chrome shows and hides—fixing classic `100vh` bugs on phones.
content:
## Problem

Legacy **`vh`** ignored URL bars and notches—layouts jumped or clipped.

## Which unit

- **svh** — smallest viewport (safe minimum).
- **lvh** — largest.
- **dvh** — dynamic; tracks live chrome movement.

## Practical

Use **`min-height: 100dvh`** for full-screen sections when baseline targets allow; pair **`safe-area-inset-*`** for notches.

title: Native CSS nesting
summary: Nest rules inside selectors with **`&`**—same mental model as Sass/Less, now valid CSS in supporting browsers.
content:
## Syntax

`&` repeats the parent compound selector; pseudo-classes nest naturally.

## Why it landed ~2024

Less preprocessor plumbing for teams that still want readable component blocks.

## Caveats

Verify **Baseline**; PostCSS can downlevel for older engines.

title: light-dark() color keyword
summary: Pick two colors for light vs dark **color schemes** without manual media queries—pairs with `color-scheme` on `:root`.
content:
## Usage

Set **`color-scheme: light dark`** so form controls pick native styling; use **`light-dark(#111, #eee)`** for author colors that flip with scheme.

## Relation

Complements **`prefers-color-scheme`**, custom properties, and **`color-mix()`**.

title: @starting-style — entry animations
summary: Define **starting styles** before an element’s first paint so transitions run when nodes appear—dialogs, popovers, route segments.
content:
## Problem

New DOM nodes often appear **without** a “from” state for `transition`.

## Pattern

Pair **`@starting-style`** with final states so the UA interpolates on insertion.

## Accessibly

Honor **`prefers-reduced-motion`**.

title: Subgrid
summary: Nested grids inherit parent track sizing so cards and rows align across columns—native alternative to brittle margin hacks.
content:
## Usage

Parent defines columns; child **`display: grid; grid-template-columns: subgrid`** participates in parent tracks.

## Support

Confirm **Baseline** before relying in marketing grids.

## Relation

Different goal than **masonry**—subgrid is about **track alignment**, not packed columns.

title: color-mix()
summary: Mix two colors in a chosen color space—tints, hover states, and accessible variants without hard-coding hex ladders.
content:
## Syntax

`color-mix(in oklab, var(--brand) 85%, white)`—prefer **perceptual spaces** (oklab/oklch) over naive sRGB when possible.

## Uses

Hover overlays, pressed buttons, alpha tricks without `rgba()` everywhere.

title: Scroll-state container queries
summary: Emerging pattern: query whether a **scroll container** or sticky child is in states like **stuck**—style headers without scroll listeners when supported.
content:
## Idea

Extend container queries with **scroll-state** conditions (e.g. sticky at top) so CSS reacts to scroll mechanics declaratively.

## Mindset

Spec + syntax **still evolving**—treat as progressive enhancement next to **Intersection Observer** fallbacks.

## Timeline

Often grouped with **2026 “CSS logic”** narratives alongside `if()` and advanced scroll APIs.

code_examples.title: Illustrative (verify shipping syntax)

title: Custom `<select>` styling (appearance / base)
summary: Standards work toward fully styleable selects—beyond trimming native chrome with border hacks. Availability varies; pair with accessible markup.
content:
## Direction

New **`appearance`** values and **parts-based** styling aim to let designers match selects to design systems while preserving semantics.

## A11y

Keep **labels**, keyboard operation, and announced roles—don’t replace with non-focusable divs.

## Support

Feature-detect; ship plain `<select>` fallback styling everywhere.

code_examples.title: Illustrative only — verify engine keywords

title: Custom CSS functions (@function)
summary: Spec work explores **author-defined functions** that return computed values—reusable math/transforms at stylesheet level. Still landing; syntax follows CSS WG drafts.
content:
## Distinction

Unlike Sass mixins, native functions integrate with the **CSS value engine** and cascade.

## Today

Use **custom properties + `calc()` / `color-mix()`** until Baseline covers your audience.

## Illustration

Articles often show `@function`-style blocks—verify against **current** spec snapshots before pasting into prod CSS.

code_examples.title: Conceptual sketch (not broadly supported yet)

title: sibling-index()
summary: Numeric index of an element among siblings—useful for staggered delays (`transition-delay`) without inline styles or nth-child hacks.
content:
## Idea

`calc(0.05s * sibling-index())` staggers list reveals.

## Support

Emerging—verify engines; fallback to **`nth-child` delays** or **JS data-index**.

## Relation

Complements **scroll-driven** and **view transition** choreography.

code_examples.title: Conceptual (verify function name in target browsers)
